hypothesis: Replacing hardcoded Sharpe normalization (7.7) with dynamic global mean and blending 0.5x Sharpe + 0.5x citation in-degree will produce a quality factor with genuine domain discrimination
method: Modified dispatch_scoring.py ucb1_score(): (1) avg_sharpe fallback from 7.7 to global_mean_sharpe, (2) sharpe_factor normalized against dynamic mean, (3) citation in-degree computed via dispatch_citation.py build_citation_indegree(), (4) cite_factor = log-scaled avg citations / global mean, (5) quality_factor = 0.5*sharpe + 0.5*citations

exploit_changes.note: domains with inflated Sharpe dropped; citation-rich domains maintained or gained

domain_citation_variation.note: citation counts show real variation across domains unlike Sharpe (CV=0.168)

results.bug_fixed: hardcoded 7.7 in sharpe_factor calculation replaced with dynamic global_mean_sharpe

status: CONFIRMED — citation blending operational, dispatch rankings shifted
